title: selliguea kamborangana
intro: Selliguea kamborangana, also known as Tawa-tawa, is a plant species native to the Philippines that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant contains a toxic compound called anatabine, which has been shown to have medicinal properties in the treatment of cancer, particularly in the inhibition of tumor growth and induction of apoptosis in cancer cells. The health benefits of Selliguea kamborangana include its potential use as an anti-cancer agent, as well as its ability to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into a tea or capsule, or used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
